explanations.1: This loads environment variables from a .env file so they can be used throughout the makefile.
explanations.4: The $$ syntax escapes the dollar sign so make passes a literal $ to the shell, allowing $(id -u) to run as a shell command that gets the current user's ID.
explanations.6: This target named 'config' depends on 'stop' running first, so when you run 'make down', it automatically stops containers before bringing them down.
explanations.13: The leading dash (-) tells make to ignore errors if these directories don't exist, so the command won't fail if there's nothing to delete.
explanations.16: The @ symbol suppresses printing the command itself to the terminal, so users only see the output, not the 'clear' command.
explanations.17: The \033 sequences are ANSI color codes that make the text yellow and bold when printed to the terminal.
explanations.39: This creates the db_data and joomla_data directories if they don't exist, using -p to silently succeed even if they're already there.
